nonBlockingIssues.description: blocking_lock() used as fallback in subscribe() and event_receiver() when try_lock() fails. Could deadlock in single-threaded runtime. Consistent with Pi transport pattern.
is_connected() returns true when Mutex is contended (conservative but briefly inaccurate). Pi transport uses Arc<AtomicBool> which is more correct.
Transient error retry test doesn't fully validate that the same request is retried with a new ID after a 429. Test validates success on fresh request instead.
probe_droid_binary uses shell-expanded $HOME in test -x argument. Could fail on non-standard shells. Falls through gracefully.
ContextTokensUpdated mapping is semantically wrong: input_tokens→used, output_tokens→limit. These are different dimensions (prompt vs completion tokens), not used/limit pair.
generate_mcp_config() is a stub — no actual file I/O or cleanup lifecycle. Config struct and serialization exist but write/cleanup is missing.
build_spawn_command() does not shell-escape string arguments. Vulnerable to breakage with special characters in model/working_dir/session_id.
connect() busy-waits with 50ms polling for init message. tokio::Notify would be cleaner but 50ms is acceptable for production.
E2E full pipeline test uses SshClient::exec() (collects all output) then manually parses lines — does not exercise the actual DroidAcpTransport IO loop.

appliedUpdates.description: Document Droid native JSON-RPC 2.0 transport and stream-json protocol in provider-transport.md

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Reconcile 'ACP transport' terminology for Droid. The mission plan says 'droid exec --output-format acp' but the actual implementation uses '--output-format stream-json'. Update mission plan references to use correct terminology: 'Droid ACP transport' uses Droid's stream-json protocol, NOT the standard ACP JSON-RPC.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: droid-acp-transport review found validation-contract.md line 678 references '--output-format acp' but actual implementation uses stream-json format. The module doc clarifies 'NOT ACP JSON-RPC' but the feature description and AGENTS.md use misleading 'ACP' terminology.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the established provider module structure convention: {protocol.rs, transport.rs, mock.rs, detection.rs} with broadcast::Sender<ProviderEvent>, Arc<Mutex<Inner>>, and io_loop background task. This pattern is now used by Pi, ACP, and Droid providers.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both reviews (droid-native-transport and droid-acp-transport) note the same structural pattern across all three provider implementations.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance about test timing fragility: provider tests use tokio::time::sleep(50-100ms) to wait for async IO loop processing. Consider recommending Notify/oneshot synchronization for new provider tests to avoid intermittent CI failures.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both droid-native-transport and droid-acp-transport tests consistently use sleep-based waits before checking received events. Same pattern in Pi transport tests.

rejectedObservations.observation: E2E tests SSH to gvps already documented in services.yaml

rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous — skill may be configured differently; not actionable from code review
